title: Houses for Rent in Edgewater, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Edgewater, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Edgewater, Cleveland's Lake Erie lakefront neighborhood? See rent ranges, transit, and how to reach our leasing team today.
answerBox: Edgewater is a West Side Cleveland neighborhood along Lake Erie, home to Edgewater Park and bordering Cudell, Detroit-Shoreway, and the city of Lakewood. House rentals directly in Edgewater are limited; citywide, comparable 2- and 3-bedroom homes typically rent between $1,100 and $1,350 a month as of mid-2026.

body: Edgewater is a West Side Cleveland neighborhood along the **Lake Erie** shoreline, bordered by **Cudell** and **Detroit-Shoreway** to the south and the city of **Lakewood** to the west. The neighborhood is best known for **Edgewater Park**, part of the Cleveland Metroparks Lakefront Reservation, which runs along the water's edge with a beach, bluff-top trails, and picnic areas open to the public.

Housing in Edgewater is a mix of early-1900s single-family homes and duplexes on residential streets set back from the park, generally similar in age and style to housing in neighboring Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway. Because it's a smaller lakefront pocket, standalone house listings directly in Edgewater are limited at any given time — [tell our leasing team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we can point you to comparable West Side homes.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house in Edgewater?
body: There's no rent index published specifically for Edgewater, so the most useful reference is Cleveland's citywide rent data by bedroom count. Per Zumper's rent report dated July 2026, the citywide median is about $1,100 a month for a 2-bedroom home and about $1,350 for a 3-bedroom, up 4.2% year-over-year. Lakefront-adjacent West Side neighborhoods like Edgewater often price toward the middle or upper half of these citywide ranges.

RentCafe's report dated July 2026 on professionally managed Cleveland apartments shows a citywide average of $1,564 a month for a unit averaging 787 square feet — a helpful ceiling reference, since that figure reflects newer managed buildings rather than the older individual houses typical of Edgewater's residential streets.

Edgewater is served by GCRTA bus routes connecting to Downtown and along the West Side lakefront corridor, and the West Shoreway (Route 2) runs along the neighborhood's southern edge, giving quick highway access into Downtown or west toward Lakewood. Edgewater Park itself has a boat launch, fishing pier, and paved paths connecting to the regional Lakefront Reservation trail system.

items: **Borders:** Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway to the south; Lakewood to the west
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes to Downtown; West Shoreway (Route 2) along the southern edge
**Outdoors:** Edgewater Park (Cleveland Metroparks Lakefront Reservation) — beach, bluff trails, boat launch
**Nearby:** Whiskey Island, a short drive east along the shoreline

body: Our current rental selection is concentrated in neighboring **Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102)** rather than directly in Edgewater, so we can't promise a specific voucher-friendly home in Edgewater itself today. Every home we work with anywhere in Greater Cleveland accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready, and our available West Side inventory changes as we add homes.

If you hold a CMHA voucher and want something near the Edgewater lakefront, [reach out and tell us what you need](/p/book-showing) — we can point you to comparable homes nearby, or see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the local voucher process works. CMHA's own [Housing Choice Voucher application page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) has current program details.

Edgewater sits between two neighborhoods where we do currently work with homes: [Detroit-Shoreway](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-detroit-shoreway-cleveland/) to the south and [Cudell](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-cudell-cleveland/) just southeast. For a full overview of rentals across every Cleveland neighborhood, see our [neighborhood guide hub](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/).

faq.q: Are there houses for rent in Edgewater, Cleveland?
faq.a: House rentals directly in Edgewater are limited because it's a smaller lakefront pocket. Most nearby inventory is in neighboring Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway. Tell our leasing team what you're looking for and we can point you to comparable West Side homes close to Edgewater.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Edgewater?
faq.a: There's no rent index specific to Edgewater, but citywide, a 2-bedroom home runs about $1,100 a month and a 3-bedroom about $1,350, per Zumper's July 2026 report. Lakefront-adjacent West Side homes often price toward the middle to upper half of these ranges.

Edgewater Park is part of the Cleveland Metroparks Lakefront Reservation along Lake Erie, with a beach, bluff-top walking trails, a boat launch, and picnic areas. It borders the residential Edgewater neighborhood on Cleveland's West Side.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with homes in Edgewater?
faq.a: Our current selection is concentrated in neighboring Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102) rather than Edgewater itself. Every home we work with anywhere in Greater Cleveland accepts Section 8 vouchers, and we add homes regularly, so it's worth telling us what you need.

faq.q: How do I get to Downtown Cleveland from Edgewater?
faq.a: The West Shoreway (Route 2) runs along Edgewater's southern edge and connects directly into Downtown Cleveland by car, and GCRTA bus routes along the West Side lakefront corridor also serve the neighborhood.
